# wp-core-base LLM Index

Primary human entry point:
- README.md

Primary agent entry point:
- AGENTS.md

Core evaluation docs:
- docs/concepts.md
- docs/support-matrix.md
- docs/faq.md
- docs/evaluation-guide.md

Core implementation docs:
- docs/getting-started.md
- docs/manifest-reference.md
- docs/downstream-usage.md
- docs/migration-guide.md
- docs/operations.md

Framework maintenance docs:
- docs/contributing.md
- docs/architecture.md
- docs/automation-overview.md
- docs/release-process.md
- docs/security-model.md

Key framework facts:
- Supports `full-core` and `content-only` downstreams.
- GitHub is required only for automated PR workflows, not for using the base itself.
- Managed dependency sources supported today: `WordPress.org`, `github-release`, and downstream-registered `premium` providers.
- The installed framework version is pinned in `.wp-core-base/framework.php`.
- Local project-owned code is first-class and must not be overwritten by updater automation.
- Runtime staging is a first-class deployment contract.
